title: Check if you can renew your passport in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Check if you can renew your passport
- Who can renew a passport
- Who can’t renew a passport
- When to renew your passport

## Who can renew a passport
You can only renew your Canadian passport if your last passport
- was an adult passport that was issued when you were at least 16 years old
- was issued within 15 years of the date you apply for your renewal See an example of where to find this information on your passport.
- See an example of where to find this information on your passport.
- was valid for 5 or 10 years
- has the same name, date of birth and place of birth as you want on your new passport
- has the same gender identifier as you want on your new passport (it can be printed on your most recent passport or have the “X” observation sticker)
All of the above must be true for you to renew your passport. You need to apply for a new passport if it’s not.
Your application will be rejected and returned if you apply for a renewal and don’t meet all the criteria.

## You can renew your passport
If all of the above are true, you can renew your passport instead of applying for a new one.

## Who can’t renew a passport
Do not apply for a renewal if you
- have a passport that was issued when you were under 16 years old You cannot renew a child passport. You need to apply for a new child passport when it expires until the child turns 16. If you’re 16 years old and your child passport has expired, you need to apply for an adult passport.
- You cannot renew a child passport. You need to apply for a new child passport when it expires until the child turns 16.
- If you’re 16 years old and your child passport has expired, you need to apply for an adult passport.
- want to change your name, date of birth, place of birth or gender identifier on the new passport
For these situations, you must apply for a new passport . You can’t renew your passport.

## When to renew your passport
- It depends on when you plan to travel. This is because each country has its own entry requirements. Always check the entry and exit requirements (opens in a new tab) for all countries you plan to visit . Then check your passport to see when it expires. This will tell you if you need to renew your passport before your trip.
- Always check the entry and exit requirements (opens in a new tab) for all countries you plan to visit .
- Then check your passport to see when it expires. This will tell you if you need to renew your passport before your trip.
- You can check our service standards (opens in a new tab) to find out approximately how long it may take us to process your application. Make sure you have your new passport before making travel plans.
- Make sure you have your new passport before making travel plans.
- If you apply to renew your passport more than a year before it expires, we’ll ask you the reason for the early renewal. If you’re submitting your application by mail, you need to include a written explanation.
- If you’re submitting your application by mail, you need to include a written explanation.
Check the date of issue. If the date it was issued is within the past 15 years and you meet the other eligibility requirements, you can apply to renew your passport.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
